note: PC hardware form factor dimensions. Most are industry/consortium specifications (ATX = Intel; PCIe = PCI-SIG; JEDEC for RAM), not formal ISO standards. All dimensions in mm unless noted.

motherboards.screw_hole_pattern: ATX motherboards use M3 × 5 mm brass standoffs on a 6.35 mm grid

form_factors.name: E-ATX
form_factors.notes: Extended ATX — common for HEDT/workstation boards

form_factors.name: ATX
form_factors.notes: Full-size desktop standard

form_factors.name: Micro-ATX
form_factors.notes: Most common mid-tower size

form_factors.name: Mini-ATX
form_factors.notes: Rare; not to be confused with Mini-ITX

form_factors.name: Mini-ITX
form_factors.notes: SFF (small form factor); single PCIe × 16 slot

form_factors.name: Nano-ITX
form_factors.notes: Embedded/industrial; soldered CPU common

form_factors.name: Pico-ITX
form_factors.notes: Embedded; ultra-compact

form_factors.name: FlexATX
form_factors.notes: Used in slim/low-profile cases

form_factors.name: SSI CEB
form_factors.notes: Server boards; SSI (Server System Infrastructure) spec

form_factors.name: SSI EEB
form_factors.notes: Server boards; same size as E-ATX but different hole positions

form_factors.name: ATX
form_factors.notes: Standard; depth varies 140–180 mm. Fan: 120 or 140 mm

form_factors.name: ATX12VO
form_factors.notes: ATX 12V Only (2019 Intel spec); 12 V single rail, fewer cables

form_factors.name: SFX
form_factors.notes: Small form factor; fan 80 mm

form_factors.name: SFX-L
form_factors.notes: SFX with 120 mm fan; higher wattage possible

form_factors.name: TFX
form_factors.notes: Thin form factor; slim tower cases

form_factors.name: 1U
form_factors.notes: Rack server 1U height

form_factors.name: 3.5" HDD
form_factors.notes: Standard desktop hard drive. 4-40 UNC or M3 mounting screws

form_factors.name: 2.5" HDD/SSD (7 mm)
form_factors.notes: Slim laptop/SSD standard height

form_factors.name: 2.5" HDD/SSD (9.5 mm)
form_factors.notes: Thicker; used in higher-capacity laptop drives

form_factors.name: 2.5" HDD (15 mm)
form_factors.notes: High-capacity enterprise 2.5" drives

form_factors.name: M.2 2230
form_factors.notes: Used in ultrabooks and the Steam Deck

form_factors.name: M.2 2242
form_factors.notes: Small laptops, embedded systems

form_factors.name: M.2 2260
form_factors.notes: Uncommon

form_factors.name: M.2 2280
form_factors.notes: Most common desktop/laptop NVMe SSD size

form_factors.name: M.2 22110
form_factors.notes: Enterprise / server M.2 drives

form_factors.name: U.2 (SFF-8639)
form_factors.notes: Enterprise NVMe; same physical size as 2.5" 15 mm

form_factors.name: EDSFF E1.S
form_factors.notes: SNIA Enterprise and Datacenter SSD Form Factor; data centre

modules.name: DDR5 DIMM
modules.notes: Standard desktop DDR5. Not backward-compatible with DDR4 slots

modules.name: DDR5 ECC RDIMM
modules.notes: Registered ECC DDR5 for server platforms; on-module power management IC

cpu_sockets.note: Socket dimensions set by CPU vendor, not ISO. Listed for reference.

sockets.name: LGA1700
sockets.notes: Intel desktop LGA; contact pads on motherboard

sockets.name: AM5
sockets.notes: AMD first LGA socket (contact pads on board)

cooling_standards.note: Cooler mounting hole patterns vary by socket.

intel_lga1700_pattern.screw: M4 into plastic backplate or M3.5 into metal standoff

amd_am4_am5_pattern.screw: M4 into metal backplate; uses same backplate for AM4/AM5
